title: UDS Span
description: A single UDS (Unified Data Substrate) observability span, shaped to the OpenTelemetry Trace Data Model (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/) and extended with SZL governance fields for SLSA attestation linkage.

trace_id.description: 128-bit globally unique trace identifier, hex-encoded (32 chars). Conforms to W3C Trace Context (https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/#trace-id). Groups all spans belonging to a single end-to-end request.

span_id.description: 64-bit unique span identifier within the trace, hex-encoded (16 chars). Conforms to the OpenTelemetry SpanContext spec (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#spancontext).

parent_span_id.description: Span ID of the parent span, or null for a root span. Used to reconstruct the directed-acyclic-graph (DAG) of spans within a trace per the OpenTelemetry Traces spec (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/signals/traces/).

component.description: The SZL mesh component that emitted the span. One of the four UDS mesh components versioned at commit 25a7e53 (uds-v0.2.0).

component_commit.description: Short-form commit SHA of the specific component release that emitted the span. Used for supply-chain traceability per SLSA provenance requirements (https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/provenance).

attestation_id.description: Opaque string identifier linking this span to a governance receipt in the DSSE-wrapped receipts file. Null when the span has not yet been attested. A non-null value indicates the span is 'well-attested' for the binary classification task defined in eval_starter.ipynb.

dsse_envelope_b64.description: Base64url-encoded DSSE (Dead Simple Signing Envelope, https://github.com/secure-systems-lab/dsse) payload. Contains the in-toto attestation predicate for this span when present. Null for unattested spans. The PAE (Pre-Authentication Encoding) format follows the DSSE v1 spec: 'DSSEv1 SP LEN(type) SP type SP LEN(payload) SP payload'.

kind.description: UDS span kind. Extends the OpenTelemetry SpanKind vocabulary (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#spankind) with UDS-specific governance and agent operation kinds. Core OTel kinds: CLIENT, SERVER, INTERNAL, PRODUCER, CONSUMER.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 UTC timestamp marking the start of the span. Aligns with OTel Span start_time_unix_nano semantics (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#span-start).

duration_ms.description: Span duration in milliseconds (float). Derived from OTel start_time_unix_nano and end_time_unix_nano. Must be >= 0.

status.description: Span status per the OpenTelemetry Status spec (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#set-status). Unset = operation completed without error; Error = error occurred; Ok = explicitly marked error-free by the developer.

status_message.description: Human-readable description of the status, populated when status is Error.
examples: Policy check failed: missing required field 'subject_sha256'

attributes.description: Key-value pairs carrying span-specific metadata. Values must be string, boolean, number, or homogeneous arrays thereof per the OTel Attributes spec (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/common/). SZL-specific keys are prefixed 'szl.'.

events.description: Timestamped annotations on the span, per the OTel SpanEvent spec (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#add-events). Useful for recording discrete governance checkpoints mid-span.

slsa_level.description: Observed SLSA level (https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/levels) at span emission time. Integer 0–3 per the SLSA v1.0 track taxonomy. Null when SLSA posture is unknown.

mesh_commit.description: The uds-mesh commit SHA at which this span was captured. Canonical anchor: github.com/szl-holdings/uds-mesh @ uds-v0.2.0 (commit 25a7e53).

split.description: Dataset split assignment: train, test, or validation.

synthetic.description: True for all records in this dataset release. All spans are synthetic-but-realistic examples generated to match the statistical distribution of production UDS mesh traffic. No live production data is included in v0.1.0.
